task.title: Verify & Harden Authentication (Frontend Route Guards + Backend Enforcement Audit)
task.description: Audit the current authentication and authorization implementation across both frontend and backend to ensure: - Frontend only handles UX-level route/page protection - Backend enforces real security for all API endpoints - No possibility of bypassing restrictions via direct API calls

objectives.objective: Verify whether protected routes on the frontend have server-side route guards (Next.js middleware or server-side redirects)
Identify places where authentication is only checked on the client after hydration
Audit backend APIs to ensure all sensitive endpoints are protected by authentication middleware
Confirm that tokens/sessions are validated on every backend request
Classify findings as UX hardening issues vs actual security vulnerabilities

acceptance_criteria.criterion: No protected frontend route relies solely on client-side guards for controlling access to sensitive data
All sensitive backend API endpoints require authentication middleware (e.g., authenticateUser)
Direct API calls cannot bypass UI restrictions
Protected routes implement server-side redirect or guard logic (middleware or server components)
Each request is validated using token/session on the backend

steps.step: Review all protected pages in page.tsx files
Identify where authentication checks are performed (client vs server)
Confirm auth checks in draft-studio.tsx and bookmarks-view.tsx occur after hydration
Check whether Next.js middleware or server-side redirect mechanisms exist
Verify sensitive routes (bookmarks, versions, notifications, articles, comments, reactions) include authenticateUser middleware
Ensure proper request flow: request → middleware → handler
Confirm backend validates token/session independently of frontend
Send direct API requests using Postman/curl without a token
Send requests with valid token but insufficient role/permissions
Verify backend responds with correct HTTP status codes (401/403)
Categorize findings into: - UX Hardening Issues (frontend-only protections) - Security Issues (backend enforcement gaps)
Propose migrating from localStorage-based auth to cookie-based or server-readable sessions
Introduce Next.js middleware or server-side redirects for protected routes
Standardize route guard patterns across the frontend

notes.note: Frontend guards are for UX only and do not provide real security
Backend authentication middleware is the primary security layer
LocalStorage-based tokens cannot be reliably used for server-side route protection
